_meta.title: Agronomy for Alex's yard — Larkmont (arid zone, ~300 m)
_meta.purpose_he: A consolidation of the collected agricultural data (soil, Kc/FAO-56, chill+GDD, pests) — filtered and tailored to Alex's five fruit crops: fig, olive, almond, pomegranate, grape. Model/literature data, not binding agronomic advice.
_meta.curated_from: harvest/agriculture/ (regional agricultural GIS, FAO-56, pomology literature, open pesticides DB) — 2026-06-09
_meta.honesty_he: All numbers are reference/model values (FAO-56, orchard literature, regional agricultural GIS layers). They are not on-site measurements. The agro-climatic table is point-specific to Larkmont; the actual chill/GDD rate depends on the year and the precise micro-climate.

sources.name_he: Regional agricultural GIS service — soil layers (regional soil groups, USDA taxonomy, evaporation, soil regimes)
FAO Irrigation & Drainage Paper 56, Table 12 — Kc crop coefficients
Pereira et al. (2024) — Kc for subtropical orchards (fig, pomegranate)
Agricultural research — dormancy breaking under deficient winter-chill conditions

site.climate_he: Arid zone with a cool winter: a winter with frosts and real chill accumulation — rare in an arid climate — alongside a very hot summer.

soil_taxonomy_USDA.desc_he: Rock outcrops, brown lithosol and loess (loessial serozem)

site.moisture_regime_he: Aridic — fully irrigation-dependent in the dry season
planting_implications_he: Deepen planting pits and break the rock layer / calcic horizon beneath.
Amend with organic matter (compost) — the local soil is nutrient-poor.
Mulch to reduce evaporation and protect roots from heat.
Drip + frequent, measured irrigation (Kc × ET₀) — shallow soil does not store water.
Choose lime/salt-tolerant rootstocks because of the calcareous soil.

kc.note_he: Crop coefficient Kc (FAO-56, single-coefficient approach). ETc = Kc × ET₀. For a young/small tree — reduce Kc according to the ground-cover percentage. On the hot, dry plateau the mid/end values tend to run high.

crops.note_he: Deciduous: Kc ≈ 0 when bare (winter). Mid range ~0.4–1.0 depending on the canopy.
Evergreen; Kc almost constant. Excess water harms oil quality.
Deciduous; Kc_mid up to ~1.0–1.1 in a mature orchard with a full canopy.
Deciduous; basal Kcb lower (~0.24/0.60/0.52). Single Kc ~0.4/0.85/0.55.
Deciduous vine; Kc_end low (controlled deficit irrigation before harvest is common).

chill_gdd.note_he: Chill hours = hours at 0–7.2°C (Weinberger model). GDD = seasonal sum of max(0,(Tmax+Tmin)/2 − Tbase). The Tbase values differ between species.
chill_gdd.context_he: Larkmont (~300 m) gets real winter chill (frosts) — rare in this arid region, most of which is chill-poor — alongside immense summer heat. When chill is lacking, growers use a dormancy-breaking agent (Dormex/cyanamide) — authorized in the database for fig and grape.

trees.fit_he: Excellent — low chill, loves heat. Two crops possible (breba + main).
Good — winter chill helps induce flowering; tolerant of drought and heat.
Good if a low-chill variety; blooms early — risk of spring frosts at altitude.
Excellent — low chill + a long, hot summer = a classic hot-zone crop.
Good — low chill, abundant heat (very hot zone); Dormex evens out bud break.
Marginal — frost-sensitive; the cold altitude is dangerous for it.
Marginal at altitude — needs extreme heat to ripen (a hotter zone is preferable).

pests.note_he: A central pest/disease for each crop + an approved organic option for use (distilled from the ~1813 rows of the pest-control database). Always check the label, dosage, and pre-harvest waiting period carefully.

organic_legend_he.COPPER: Copper compound (allowed in organic, with caution)
